items.title: USAG Italy ACS Celebrates 61st Birthday Broll
items.description: Army Community Service (ACS) celebrated its 61st birthday with the Vicenza Military Community and U.S. Army Garrison Italy at the ACS lobby on Caserma Ederle, Italy, July 15, 2026. ACS was founded on July 25, 1965, with a mission of “Enhancing the Well-being of Military Communities by Fostering Resilience and Providing Comprehensive Support.” 61 years later ACS continues to serve military communities worldwide. (U.S. Army Video by Sgt. Salvador Castro)

items.title: 32nd iteration of CARAT Thailand concludes with stronger maritime partnerships
items.description: The United States concluded a landmark 32nd iteration of Cooperation Afloat Readiness and Training (CARAT) Thailand, July 15, 2026, in Songkhla, reinforcing America's enduring commitment to a free, open, and secure Indo-Pacific.

items.title: Hero to Hero: Sgt Gerard Elfering: B-Roll
items.description: U.S. Army Sgt. Gerard Elfering, Allied Trade Specialist with the 1-1 Air Defense Artillery, speaks about his actions during a flash flood in which he shuttled eight people across flooded streets on a paddleboard on Camp Shields, Okinawa June 15, 2026. This video highlighted the efforts he put in with footage taken from a cell phone showing him assisting a mother and daughter. (U.S. Navy video by Mass Communications Specialist Petty Officer 3rd Class Tyler D. Jackson)

items.title: B-roll: AFRICOM kicks off Inaugural West Africa Religious Affairs Symposium
items.description: B-roll footage during the West Africa Religious Affairs Symposium (WARAS) in Accra, Ghana, July 14, 2026. During the 3-day exchange, U.S. and African military chaplains from 10 partner nations shared best practices and experiences in providing service members the spiritual and emotional support they need. (U.S. Air Force photo by Staff Sgt. Krystal England)

items.title: B-roll: West Africa Religious Affairs Symposium (WARAS)
items.description: U.S. service members and African partner nation chaplains participate in the West Africa Religious Affairs Symposium (WARAS) at the Ghana Air Force Aviation College, Burma Camp, Accra, Ghana, July 15, 2026. U.S. and African military chaplains from 10 partner nations gathered for the three-day exchange to discuss methods to support and strengthen service members' spiritual resiliency. (U.S. Air Force video by Airman 1st Class Alexandra Dale)

items.title: Navy to start degassing 12th Red Hill facility tank, continues air quality monitoring
items.description: JOINT BASE PEARL HARBOR-HICKAM, Hawaii -- Navy Closure Task Force-Red Hill (NCTF-RH) will begin degassing tank 16 at the Red Hill Bulk Fuel Storage Facility (RHBFSF) this week, marking another significant milestone in the facility's decommissioning process. Tank decommissioning is a multi-stage process involving defueling, venting (degassing), cleaning, and ultimately, the permanent closure of each tank.
items.unit_name: Navy Closure Task Force - Red Hill

items.title: 3d AA Bn. conducts amphibious refamiliarization exercises
items.description: U.S. Marines with Bravo Company, 3d Assault Amphibian Battalion, 1st Marine Division, conduct Amphibious Combat Vehicle operations at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California, June 12, 2026. Amphibious vehicle crews conduct diverse training evolutions across both land and sea, strengthening their ability to operate effectively in austere, complex, and turbulent battlespaces. (U.S. Marine Corps video by Lance Cpl. Samuel Krause)

items.title: La. Guard supports St. Tammany Parish coastal restoration with Christmas tree drop
items.description: MADISONVILLE, La. – Aviators with the Louisiana National Guard's 1st Assault Battalion, 244th Aviation Regiment, partnered with St. Tammany Parish Government and Coastal Environments, Inc. to support a coastal restoration project by conducting a Christmas tree drop along the north shore of Lake Pontchartrain near Madisonville, Louisiana, July 8.

items.title: NAVFAC Washington Contracting Product Service Division Engages Leadership to Strengthen Warfighter Readiness

items.title: 'It's About People' : Sergeant Major Retires After 36 Years of Military Service
items.description:
Sgt. Maj. Jeffrey C. Sima spent 36 years wearing the Army uniform, but when asked what mattered most, his answer wasn't a rank, an assignment or a deployment.
"It's about people," he told family, friends, fellow Soldiers and military leaders gathered during his retirement ceremony, July 11, at the Illinois Army National Guard Marseilles Armory . "If I made a difference in even one Soldier's life or career, then every sacrifice was worth it."

This year marks the historic 10th anniversary of the partnership between the U.S. Marine Corps and USA Wrestling. Through this collaboration, the Marine Corps actively engages with coaches, athletes, and community leaders nationwide. (U.S. Marine Corps video by Sgt. Aidan Hekker)

items.title: NAVSUP FLC Pearl Harbor Welcomes New Commanding Officer During Change of Command Ceremony
items.description: Naval Supply Systems Command Fleet Logistics Center Pearl Harbor will hold a change of command ceremony Friday, July 10, 2026, at 10 a.m. on Kilo Pier aboard Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam.  Capt. Sean M. Andrews will relinquish command of NAVSUP FLC Pearl Harbor to Capt.  Anthony P. Bannister during a ceremony presided over by Rear Adm. Kevin M. Corcoran, deputy commander, Naval Supply Systems Command.
items.location_display: Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam, Hawaii, United States

items.title: 1st Marine Regiment Relief and Appointment
items.description: U.S. Marines with 1st Battalion, 1st Marine Regiment, 1st Marine Division, hold a relief and appointment ceremony at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California, July 10, 2026. During the ceremony, Sgt. Maj. Tyrone J. Mccullough relinquished his duty as Battalion Sergeant Major to Sgt. Maj. Austin B. Rice. Mccullough is a native of Michigan. Rice is a native of Texas. (U.S. Marine Corps video by Lance Cpl. Ricardo DelCastillo and Lance Cpl. Dylan Jameson)
items.credit: LCpl Diego Berumen and LCpl Ricardo DelCastillo and LCpl Dylan Jameson

items.title: 11th Marines holds a change of command ceremony
items.description: U.S. Marines with 11th Marine Regiment, 1st Marine Division, hold a change of command ceremony at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California, July 10, 2026. During the ceremony, Col. Benjamin B. Harrison, relinquished command of the regiment to Col. Steven C. Allshouse. Harrison is a native of California. Allshouse is a native of New Jersey. (U.S. Marine Corps video by Sgt. Anita Ramos and Lance Cpl. Samuel Krause.)
items.credit: LCpl Samuel Krause and Sgt Anita Ramos

items.title: Lyster welcomes new commander
items.description: FORT RUCKER, Ala. — Lyster Army Health Clinic welcomed incoming commander Lt. Col. Ryan Allred and bid farewell to Lt. Col. Leah Steder during a change of command ceremony at the Army Aviation Museum at Fort Rucker on July 9, 2026.

items.title: MCRD San Diego Fox Company Graduation
items.description: The new Marines of Lima Company, 3rd Recruit Training Battalion, graduated from Marine Corps Recruit Depot San Diego, July 10th 2026. Graduation took place at the completion of the 13-week transformation which included training for drill, marksmanship, basic combat skills, and Marine Corps customs and traditions. Following graduation, the Marines were given 10 days of leave before taking the next step in training at the School of Infantry at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California.
items.unit_name: Marine Corps Recruit Depot, San Diego

items.title: NUWC Division, Keyport Employee Spotlight: Alex Read
items.description: Sustaining our warfighting advantage requires a dedicated team of experts committed to Fleet readiness. Among these experts is Mechanical Engineer Alex Read, a standout employee known for his work ethic, positive attitude and deep expertise in robotics and rapid prototyping.
items.unit_name: Naval Undersea Warfare Center Division Keyport

items.title: NAVELSG Sailors Support Fleet Week New York, Showcase Expeditionary Logistics During America 250 Celebration
items.description: Throughout the week, NAVELSG Sailors represented both NAVELSG and NECC at interactive displays, speaking with members of the public about the command’s role in enabling naval operations around the globe. The team highlighted expeditionary cargo handling, logistics-over-the-shore operations, port opening capabilities and the specialized mission sets that allow the Navy to rearm, refuel and resupply forces in contested and austere environments.
items.location_display: New York, New York, United States

items.title: Press Release: Capt. Kent Davis Assumes Command of Recruit Training Command
items.description: Capt. Kent Davis relieved Capt. Ken Froberg as commanding officer of Recruit Training Command (RTC) during a traditional change of command ceremony held July 10 in the USS Midway Ceremonial Drill Hall at RTC, the Navy's only boot camp, at Naval Station Great Lakes.

items.title: Coast Guard Cutter Waesche holds change-of-command ceremony
items.description: ALAMEDA, Calif. — The U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Waesche (WMSL 751) held a change-of-command ceremony at Base Alameda on Thursday. Vice Adm. Joseph Buzzella, commander, Coast Guard Pacific Area, presided over the ceremony in which Capt. Dave Ratner relieved Capt. Tyson Scofield as Waesche’s commanding officer.

items.title: Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star holds change-of-command ceremony
items.description: SEATTLE – The U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star (WAGB 10) held a change-of-command ceremony on June 29 at Base Seattle. Rear Adm. Jeffrey Novak, deputy commander of Coast Guard Pacific Area and commander of Coast Guard District Southwest, presided over the ceremony in which Capt. Justin Vanden Heuvel relieved Capt. Jeffery Rasnake as the Polar Star’s commanding officer.

items.title: USS Lassen Departs Eastport, Maine, Following Successful Port Visit
items.description:
EASTPORT, Maine (July 6, 2026) — The Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Lassen (DDG 82) departed Eastport, Maine, July 6, following a successful port visit to participate in the city's annual Fourth of July festivities.

Story by Lt. Chad Dougless, USS Lassen

items.title: USS Wichita Departs Bristol, Rhode Island, Following Successful Port Visit
items.description:
BRISTOL, R.I. (July 5, 2026) — The Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Wichita (LCS 13) departed Bristol, Rhode Island, July 5, following a successful port visit to participate in the city's 241st annual Fourth of July festivities.

By Lt. Cmdr. Alison Mondville, USS Wichita Public Affairs
items.unit_name: Commander, Littoral Combat Ship Squadron TWO

items.title: COL. JEFFREY HICKS APPOINTED AS UNITED STATES PROPERTY AND FISCAL OFFICER FOR ILLINOIS
items.description:
Maj. Gen. Rodney Boyd, The Adjutant General of Illinois and Commander of the Illinois National Guard, has announced the appointment of Col. Jeffrey P. Hicks as the United States Property and Fiscal Officer (USPFO) for Illinois. 
Col. Hicks will replace Col. Brian Creech, who is retiring at the end of September after a 40-year distinguished career.
items.unit_name: Joint Force Headquarters - Illinois National Guard Public Affairs

items.title: USS San Antonio Assumes Relief Mission in Venezuela
items.description: Demonstrating an unwavering commitment to ongoing disaster relief efforts, the amphibious transport dock USS San Antonio (LPD 17) arrived pierside in La Guaira on Thursday, July 9, 2026. The San Antonio seamlessly relieved the USS Fort Lauderdale (LPD 28) as the primary maritime component center in support of the Department of State-led humanitarian assistance mission following the devastating June 24 earthquakes.
items.unit_name: U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command / U.S. 4th Fleet

items.title: Vice Chief of Naval Operations talks to U.S. Naval War College students, faculty
items.description: Vice Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Jim Kilby visited the U.S. Naval War College in Newport, Rhode Island, on July 10, 2026. Kilby spoke to war college leadership and officers enrolled in the College of Maritime Operational Warfare’s (CMOW) Maritime Staff Operators Course (MSOC) while at the institution’s campus.

items.title: NUWC Division Newport’s 2025 Annual Overview highlights technical excellence, strategic vision
items.description: The Strategic Planning Office and the Public Affairs Office gathered stories and photos that highlight the people, projects, and events that made for an eventful 2025 at Division Newport. The overview discusses the origin of the “Mastery of the Seas at All Depths: 2035” strategic vision, command metrics, and offers a month-by-month recap of Division Newport highlights.

items.title: NAS JRB Fort Worth Modernizes Airfield Safety with New Precision Radar
items.description: NAS JRB Fort Worth, Texas — Naval Air Station (NAS) Joint Reserve Base (JRB) Fort Worth has taken a major step forward in aviation safety and operational readiness with the installation of a new AN/FPN-68A Precision Approach Radar. The modern solid-state system replaces the decades-old AN/FPN-63, delivering enhanced digital capabilities that will improve aircraft recovery in challenging Texas weather while significantly reducing maintenance demands.
